title: alchemilla glaberrima subsp. major
intro: Alchemilla glaberrima subsp. major, also known as a type of lady's mantle, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its large, rounded leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been found to have a number of medicinal applications, including the treatment of wounds, diarrhea, and menstrual disorders. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla glaberrima subsp. major are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to a range of health benefits, including the protection of the skin, the promotion of wound healing, and the regulation of the menstrual cycle. Alchemilla glaberrima subsp. major can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
